failures.description: The caller violated a language binding API precondition, such as passing a null required pointer. It is not a wire failure.
A decimal integer string is empty, signed, padded, fractional, or contains non-digits.
A uint64 representation exceeds 20 UTF-8 bytes or its canonical decimal value exceeds 18446744073709551615; the byte-length check precedes lexical validation.
A binding received a cyclic in-memory value that cannot be represented as JSON.
A string contains invalid Unicode, including an unpaired surrogate.
A number is NaN or infinite and therefore is not a JSON number.
A finite binary64 value is integer-valued outside the safe-integer range and its RFC 8785 serialization does not retain a decimal point; use a contract-declared string representation.
A binding received a language-specific value that has no JSON representation.
Raw JSON is malformed; a binding should report an offset or path when available.
A closed core object contains an undeclared field.
Input exceeds a declared byte, depth, string, member, array, or token bound.
Raw JSON contains the same object member name more than once.
A previously observed durable position identity is reused with a different stable batch_id or business digest binding.
A digest does not use the required sha256 lowercase hexadecimal representation.
A declared business digest differs from the digest computed over the frozen canonical projection.
A message is bound to a gateway, session, epoch, or credential generation that is no longer current.
A message declares expires_at_ms earlier than sent_at_ms.
The explicit evaluation_time_ms is equal to or later than expires_at_ms.
Data-loss evidence violates first_lost_position <= last_lost_position < earliest_retained_position.
One message repeats the same stream_id and stream_epoch cursor identity.
The declared protocol or contract version is not supported by the selected profile.
The alpha contract deliberately has no production shared-Broker authentication transcript.
A capability declaration does not grant execution; the governed job or edge policy denied it.
A Thing Model attempted to declare direct execution instead of a governed job.
A key is reused within or across the property, point, or capability namespaces of one Thing Model, or a parameter key is repeated within one capability.
The requested property key is absent from the supplied Thing Model.
The requested point key is absent from the supplied Thing Model.
The requested capability key is absent from the supplied Thing Model.
